_schema: Epistemic state tracking — what each character knows/doesn't know/falsely believes at each chapter boundary. Updated after each draft. Queried by /forge-write during context loading.
_usage: Before writing chapter N, load the entry for chapter N-1 to know what the POV character is aware of. After drafting, update with learned_this_chapter.

knows: Normal life on the ranch
Something catastrophic just happened (the Integration)
Rex is with him
The world has changed — sky, sounds, everything wrong
The Integration happened — reality reshaped
A System exists and is speaking to him
He has been 'integrated' — something was done to him
Rex survived and is bonded to him
He's in some kind of Tutorial
doesnt_know: The System is binding him progressively
falsely_believes: The System is a neutral tool

knows: Flint exists — an AI companion assigned to him
Flint's name (F.L.I.N.T.)
He has a HUD overlay (basic phase)
Structural Analysis is his primary skill
The Tutorial has rooms to clear
Rex can sense danger through their bond
doesnt_know: Flint has network access to System data
falsely_believes: The System is a neutral tool helping him survive
Flint is fully loyal to him (Flint serves the System first)
learned_this_chapter: Flint exists and has a name

knows: Tutorial rooms have monsters and objectives
SA reveals structural weak points in enemies and environment
His crossbow and hammer are effective tools
Rex fights alongside him — physical combat, not magical
The bond with Rex transmits emotional impressions
learned_this_chapter: Rex bond transmits impressions in danger

knows: Combat is lethal — he can die here
Killing is necessary for survival
His abilities are growing with use
The Tutorial is testing him specifically
Rex's combat instincts are reliable
learned_this_chapter: He can kill when he has to

knows: Josie Pickett exists — met her in the Tutorial
Josie is an Appraiser class
Other humans survived the Integration and are in Tutorials
The rooms get harder
Flint has access to System information Nate can't see
doesnt_know: The full party that will form
falsely_believes: Flint is fully on his side

knows: Marcus Webb exists — military background, Vanguard class
Working together is necessary to survive harder rooms
The Tutorial is a gauntlet with escalating difficulty
Team tactics matter — his SA + Marcus's combat + Josie's appraisal
Rex and the team can coordinate
doesnt_know: The full scope of post-Tutorial world
learned_this_chapter: Marcus Webb exists and is Vanguard

knows: The Tutorial has a final challenge (Room 10 boss)
The team can fight together effectively
His abilities have grown significantly
The bond with Rex is deepening
Post-Tutorial world awaits — and it won't be safe
doesnt_know: What the post-Tutorial world actually looks like
falsely_believes: Graduating the Tutorial is the hard part
learned_this_chapter: The Tutorial has a boss fight
The team is stronger together than apart

flint._note: Flint knows far more than Nate — he has System network access. Track what he REVEALS vs what he WITHHOLDS.
withholds_through_ch08: Nate's hidden stats and origin markers
The full Tutorial structure before Nate reaches each room
has_revealed_by_ch08: Room-level tactical data (enemy types, weak points)
Other party members' class info when asked

reader._note: Track dramatic irony — what the READER knows that Nate doesn't. Built through narrator hints and interludes.
knows_that_nate_doesnt_by_ch08: The System is not neutral (narrator hints throughout)
The Integration is global, not local
